codeReview.summary: The implementation successfully delivers on the feature's objectives: 13 design system components were bridged into production, CSS variables replaced hardcoded hex colors in auth pages, Badge component standardized in factory pages, and HR/Payroll navigation/routes removed. TypeScript compiles clean and all 308 tests pass. One minor non-blocking issue found: ProfilePage.tsx still contains hardcoded hex colors (#10b981) that were not addressed, though this page was not explicitly listed in the feature requirements.

issues.description: Hardcoded hex color #10b981 remains in MFA status icon and copy success checkmark. Feature description specified 6 auth pages (AuthLayout, LoginPage, MfaPage, ForgotPasswordPage, ResetPasswordPage, FirstPasswordChangePage) but ProfilePage was not included. The colors should use var(--color-success-icon) for consistency, but this is outside the explicit scope of this feature.
KanbanBoard tag uses hardcoded color: '#fff' for text color. This is an edge case where the tag.color is a custom user-provided color, so the white text is intentional for contrast. Acceptable as-is.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker suggested adding a note in the frontend-worker skill about the full bun run test --run suite having a known timeout issue. The worker found targeted vitest runs (npx vitest run src/components/ui/__tests__) are more reliable for validation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'Consider adding a note in the skill that the full bun run test --run suite has a known timeout issue — targeted vitest runs are more reliable for validation.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: CSS variable usage is now consistently enforced across auth pages. The pattern established by this worker (replacing #hex with var(--color-*) ) should be documented as a coding standard for future workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AuthLayout.tsx, LoginPage.tsx, MfaPage.tsx, ForgotPasswordPage.tsx, ResetPasswordPage.tsx, FirstPasswordChangePage.tsx all now use CSS variables exclusively for colors.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Badge standardization pattern established: statusBadgeVariant() helper function maps domain status strings to Badge variant props ('success' | 'warning' | 'danger' | 'info' | 'default'). This pattern should be documented for future workers implementing status displays.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ProductionLogsPage.tsx and ProductionPlansPage.tsx both implement statusBadgeVariant() helper that returns Badge variant strings, replacing the previous inline clsx+statusBadgeClass pattern.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The HR/Payroll module is marked 'on hold' per AGENTS.md, and this feature correctly removed HR/Payroll navigation and routes. Future workers should be aware that HR/Payroll backend endpoints exist but the module should not be built.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AccountingLayout.tsx: HR & Payroll NavGroup removed, MODULE_ROUTES cleared. App.tsx: EmployeesPage, AttendancePage, LeaveRequestsPage, PayrollPage lazy imports and routes removed.

summary: PASS. The foundation-design-system-bridge feature is well-implemented. All 13 design system components were successfully bridged with proper CSS variable usage. Auth pages (the 6 specified) now use CSS variables exclusively. Badge component is properly standardized in factory pages with clean variant mapping. HR/Payroll navigation and routes have been cleanly removed. TypeScript compiles clean and 308 tests pass. One non-blocking observation: ProfilePage.tsx has remaining hardcoded colors but was outside explicit scope. The worker followed the skill procedure well and provided useful feedback about test timeout issues for future improvements.
